mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.5%, it signals a rapid normalization of the labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, driving immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business sector demand expansion. This dual surge validates earnings growth trajectories, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02 compared to single-proxy models, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial capacity, a rise in the industrial production index triggers supply-side inflation expectations that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with tight capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO rises above 102.0 during capacity-constrained periods, or the negative correlation disappears when CPIAUCSL is below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust economic momentum, prompting institutional rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered windows, or predictive power vanishes when adjusted for concurrent FEDFUNDS hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and core inflation indicate a disinflationary monetary easing environment. This combination reduces real borrowing costs while preserving purchasing power, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate statistically significant positive returns (p < 0.05) over 60-day windows, or forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL declines faster than FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying corporate hiring demand, which directly correlates with rising profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative PAYEMS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A break below the 100.0 level in the industrial production index signals a supply contraction that triggers defensive positioning by institutional investors, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive returns in more than 65% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the signal shows no incremental predictive value over UNRATE movements.
